element_introduction_order.element: Dynamic track paths (multiple iterations with branching)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Queen League (Death Wind, Port Town, Red Canyon)

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching pit stop for energy replenishment
Accumulating Super Jet boost (visual indicator green)
Maintaining required placement for disqualification avoidance
typical_tension_triggers: 5-lap race structure with escalating placement requirements
Proximity to hazards (slip zones, magnets, mines)
Risk of falling off track edges
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: High-speed sustained tension with lap-based relief cycles; each lap completion provides speed boost reward but maintains pressure through mandatory placement improvement requirement

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Practice mode allows player to attempt 7 selected courses without Grand Prix pressure, signaling optional skill-building paths

difficulty_curve.early_game: Knight League offers four difficulty levels (beginner, standard, expert, master) with foundational hazard and mechanic introduction
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Queen League introduces multiple-iteration tracks (Death Wind, Port Town, Red Canyon) with dynamic pathways that shift between lap iterations, increasing spatial complexity and decision-making
difficulty_curve.late_game: King League escalates challenge with master-difficulty options and highest-complexity track configurations
notable_spikes: Transition from Knight to Queen League: introduction of dynamic branching pathways
Master difficulty unlock: requires mastery of all standard mechanics under maximum pressure
Mute City variations (day/evening/night): configuration changes add novelty complexity

notable_level_patterns.description: Death Wind, Port Town, and Red Canyon feature alternate pathways that are only accessible on specific lap iterations. A path available on lap 1 closes on lap 2, and vice versa. This creates conditional spatial grammar where player choice locks out alternative routes, forcing commitment to a single line per lap.

Three iterations of Mute City present identical track with day, evening, and night settings plus slightly altered configurations. Creates visual variety and minor layout changes without altering core mechanics, introducing players to theme variations early.

15 tracks divided into Knight (beginner-friendly), Queen (intermediate hazards), and King (expert) leagues. Each league can be attempted at four difficulty levels independently, allowing players to master spatial patterns before increasing mechanical difficulty.
